holdings.name: Mastercard
holdings.thesis_summary: Mastercard is one half of the global payment network duopoly — 4.5B+ cards and 150M+ merchant acceptance points create a two-sided network moat compounding annually as cash is globally displaced. The Value-Added Services segment (cybersecurity, fraud analytics, data, loyalty) grew +22%/+18% cc in Q1 2026 and is the key differentiator driving margin expansion beyond pure transaction fees. BVNK ($1.8B) and Agent Pay position MA as stablecoin and AI-agent infrastructure rather than a disruption victim; watch cross-border volume (~30% of net revenue) and whether the BVNK integration delivers on its strategic bet.

EU/US interchange regulation — EU caps already at 0.2-0.3% for consumer cards; if commercial card or cross-border fee caps spread, revenue per transaction declines structurally

holdings.name: Amazon
holdings.thesis_summary: Amazon is a three-engine flywheel — AWS ($37.6B/qtr, +28% YoY, fastest in 15 quarters; AI revenue run rate >$15B), Advertising (~$15B/qtr growing ~18% YoY at high margin), and Prime ecosystem (retail conversion engine, >200M members) — with each engine reinforcing the others. Q1 2026 confirmed margin expansion is structural: record 13.1% operating margin as AWS mix and advertising operating leverage overwhelm retail cost pressures, even with $200B FY2026 capex (the largest single-year corporate infrastructure bet in history). The capex is not speculative — customer commitments for a significant portion exist, with OpenAI's >$100B commitment as one example; AWS's 28% growth at this scale (CEO Jassy: 'very unusual for a business to grow this fast on a base this large') validates the bet. Watch: Q2 2026 consumer spending in retail amid tariff-driven price inflation (Amazon prices +12.8% vs Walmart 5.3%), and whether AWS sustains 25%+ growth as Google Cloud and Azure close ground.
key_risks: Tariff-driven retail headwind — marketplace average prices +12.8% from tariffs (vs Walmart 5.3%); third-party seller buffer stocks exhausted; consumer price sensitivity in discretionary categories could dampen GMV growth in H2 2026; FBA fee increases add to seller cost pressures

FY2026 capex ~$200B creates near-zero FCF for 2026-2027 — doubled from 2025's $131B; ROI depends on AWS demand materialising at the pace of infrastructure build; any AWS growth deceleration below 20% would signal capex overshoot

AWS competition intensifying — Google Cloud +63% YoY in Q1 2026 (vs AWS +28%); Azure growing; at $150B+ annualised AWS revenue, sustaining 25%+ growth requires winning increasingly large enterprise workloads against well-capitalised competitors

holdings.name: Philip Morris International
holdings.thesis_summary: Philip Morris International is executing the world's largest tobacco-to-smoke-free transition — IQOS commands 76% of the global heat-not-burn market (43M+ consumers, 106 markets) and in Q1 2026 overtook Marlboro as PM's #1 brand by volume, a landmark milestone. ZYN (FDA-authorized January 2026 for 20 products) controls 70% of the US nicotine pouch market ($3.24B in FY2025, +37% YoY), making PM the clear regulator-friendly winner in the nicotine alternatives transition. The 2026-2028 growth algorithm targets 9-11% adjusted EPS CAGR and 6-8% organic revenue CAGR — already delivered in 2 of 3 years ahead of the prior 2024-2026 algorithm. Watch: FDA MRTP decision on ZYN (could allow 'less harmful' marketing), IQOS US rollout under the new PMI U.S. business unit, and debt reduction toward management's preferred <2x net debt/EBITDA.
key_risks: FDA regulatory reversal on ZYN — the FDA authorized 20 products in January 2026 but MRTP (modified risk) designation is still pending; any reversal of authorization, flavour ban, or adverse ZYN Ultra ruling would directly hit the core US growth engine

FX headwinds structural — PM earns most revenues in non-USD currencies and reports in USD; dollar strength meaningfully suppresses reported EPS despite strong organic growth (Q1 2026: adj EPS +16% but reported EPS -9.3% YoY)

holdings.name: Microsoft
holdings.thesis_summary: Microsoft is the world's most complete enterprise technology stack — Office 365 (400M+ commercial users), Azure (second-largest cloud at +40% cc growth), GitHub (100M+ developers), Teams, LinkedIn, and Copilot create layered moats where enterprise switching cost is effectively infinite for large organizations. The AI business crossed a $37B annualized run rate in Q3 FY2026 while Copilot paid seats grew 33% sequentially to 20M — monetization is real and accelerating even though 20M seats against 400M+ eligible users means the Copilot upsell is still in the first innings. Azure acceleration (+40% cc in Q3 vs +38% Q2) is the key leading indicator: each Azure infrastructure commitment is a 5-10 year relationship. Watch: whether Copilot seats exceed 50M in FY2027 (thesis-confirming) and whether FCF recovers from the capex trough in 2027-28 as management has guided.

## META — Q1 2026 Earnings Brief
Report date: April 29, 2026

### Executive Summary
| Metric | Actual | Estimate | Delta |
|---|---|---|---|
| Revenue | $56.31B | $55.4B | +1.6% beat |
| Revenue growth (YoY) | +33% | — | Accelerating from +21% in Q4 2025 |
| EPS (GAAP diluted) | $10.44 | $6.67 | +57% beat* |
| EPS (adjusted, ex-tax benefit) | ~$7.31 | — | — |
| Operating Income | $22.87B | — | +30% YoY |
| Operating Margin | 40.5% | — | vs 38.0% Q1 2025 |
| Family DAP | 3.56B | — | +4% YoY |

*EPS includes $8.03B one-time tax benefit ("One Big Beautiful Bill Act"). Adjusted EPS ~$7.31. Headline beat substantially inflated.

### Segment Breakdown
- Family of Apps: $55.91B revenue; advertising $55.02B (+33% YoY); ad impressions +19% YoY; price per ad +~12% YoY — volume and price compounding simultaneously
- Family DAP: 3.56B (+4% YoY); QoQ dip vs 3.61B Q4 2025 — management confirmed geopolitical exclusions (Iran/Russia), not structural
- Reality Labs: $402M revenue; operating loss -$4.03B (run rate ~$16B/yr); Orion AR glasses in limited commercial testing; no monetization timeline

### Guidance Delta
Q2 2026 guidance: $58-61B revenue (midpoint $59.5B, +15-18% YoY). FY2026 capex raised again to $125-145B from $115-135B; multi-year infrastructure commitments jumped $107B in Q1 alone; CoreWeave $21B deal through 2032 locked in. FCF near-zero in 2026. Total FY2026 expenses guided $113-123B. Management tone bullish on AI ROI; Zuckerberg framed capex as "once in a generation" infrastructure opportunity.

### Key Quotes
Zuckerberg: "WhatsApp Business AI went from 1 million weekly conversations to 10 million in just the first few months of this year."

Zuckerberg: "Our AI studio now has more than 1.4 million monthly active creators, up more than 3x since the beginning of this year."

Susan Li (CFO): "We now expect 2026 total expenses to be in the range of $113-123 billion."

### Thesis Check
Reinforces the bull case. Revenue +33% is genuine — ad impressions +19% and price per ad +12% compounding simultaneously is rare and powerful. The AI monetization loop is activating: WhatsApp Business 10x in 4 months, Meta AI scaling. Q2 guidance +15-18% confirms the core ad engine is healthy even as comps get tougher. The capex raise is the only meaningful negative: $125-145B means near-zero FCF in 2026 and AI ROI is unproven at this scale. Thesis: intact and reinforced on the revenue side. Capex ROI is the single variable to watch.

### Red Flags
- EPS quality: GAAP $10.44 includes $8.03B one-time tax benefit; adjusted ~$7.31 — do not compare headline to prior periods
- CapEx raised again to $125-145B FY2026 (from $115-135B); multi-year commitments +$107B in Q1; FCF near-zero; AI ROI timeline unproven at this scale
- DAP QoQ dip: 3.56B vs 3.61B Q4 2025 — management cites Iran/Russia exclusions; watch Q2 for recovery confirmation
- Stock -7% after-hours on capex raise — market skeptical on unproven AI spend

## GOOGL — Q1 2026 Earnings Brief
Report date: April 29, 2026

### Executive Summary
| Metric | Actual | Estimate | Delta |
|---|---|---|---|
| Revenue | $109.9B | $107.2B | +2.5% beat |
| Revenue growth (YoY) | +22% | — | 11th consecutive double-digit quarter |
| EPS (GAAP diluted) | $5.11 | $2.64 | +93% beat* |
| Operating Income | $39.7B | — | +30% YoY |
| Operating Margin | 36.1% | — | +220bps YoY |
| Free Cash Flow | $10.1B | — | OCF $45.8B; CapEx drag severe |

*EPS includes ~$2.35/share non-cash equity securities gains. Core operating EPS ~$2.76. Headline beat substantially inflated.

### Segment Breakdown
| Segment | Revenue | YoY | Note |
|---|---|---|---|
| Search & other | $60.4B | +19% | AI Overviews driving query growth; queries at all-time high |
| YouTube ads | $9.88B | +11% | Decelerating vs Q4; still solid |
| Google Network | $6.97B | -4% | Structural decline continues |
| Subscriptions / Platforms / Devices | $12.38B | +19% | Play + Workspace + hardware |
| Google Services total | $89.64B | +16% | Op margin 45.3% (+300bps) |
| Google Cloud | $20.0B | +63% | Op income $6.6B; margin 32.9% vs 17.8%; backlog $462B |
| Other Bets | $411M | -9% | Immaterial; declining |

### Guidance Delta
CapEx guidance raised to $180-190B for 2026 (from $175-185B), with 2027 flagged as "significantly higher." FCF severely compressed — Q1 FCF $10.1B vs $39.7B operating income (25% conversion). Wiz acquisition creates low single-digit % headwind to Cloud margins for rest of 2026. No revenue guidance given. Management tone confident but acknowledged near-term compute constraints limiting Cloud upside.

### Key Quotes
Sundar Pichai: "We are compute constrained in the near term. Our cloud revenue would have been higher if able to meet demand."

Sundar Pichai: "We're the only provider to offer first-party solutions across the entire enterprise AI stack."

Anat Ashkenazi: "Higher depreciation expense and related data center operations costs such as energy will pressure profitability going forward."

### Thesis Check
Reinforces the bull case on Cloud and Search moat, but capital intensity is the new core risk. Cloud at $20B (+63%) with 32.9% op margin is exceptional. Search +19% demolishes AI-cannibalization narrative for now. The problem: $180-190B CapEx in 2026, rising in 2027. Near-zero FCF despite $39.7B operating income. Thesis holds if AI infrastructure spend yields durable Cloud share gains — but ROI is unproven at this scale.

### Red Flags
- EPS quality: Headline $5.11 includes ~$2.35/share non-cash gains. Core ~$2.76.
- FCF compression: $10.1B FCF on $45.8B OCF. CapEx absorbing ~78% of operating cash.
- Compute constraint admitted: capacity bottleneck limits near-term Cloud growth ceiling.
- Wiz headwind: Cloud margin steps back Q2-Q4 2026; 32.9% is likely the peak for now.
- Antitrust: DOJ cases unresolved. Structural breakup decision expected late 2026.
